chambers.name: Chamber of the Eye
chambers.subtitle: God of the Sun
chambers.introText:
You dare enter the tomb of the Pharaohs?
The Eye of Ra watches your every step.
Answer wisely... or face the consequences.
chambers.completeText: The Eye is satisfied. You have earned your passage, explorer.

questions.text: Which river was the lifeblood of ancient Egyptian civilisation?
questions.correctReaction: Even Ra's light cannot match the Nile's power. Correct.
questions.wrongReaction: The Eye closes. You do not know your history.
questions.trapText: The Eye closes. You do not know your history.

questions.text: Ancient Egypt lasted for over 3,000 years.
questions.correctReaction: 3,000 years of glory. The sun witnessed every one.
questions.wrongReaction: Ra turns away. Study before you enter sacred ground.
questions.trapText: Ra turns away. Study before you enter sacred ground.

questions.text: What writing system did ancient Egyptians use?
questions.correctReaction: Sacred writing. You read the walls well.
questions.wrongReaction: You cannot read the walls. The Eye is disappointed.
questions.trapText: You cannot read the walls. The Eye is disappointed.

questions.text: Cleopatra was Egyptian by birth.
questions.correctReaction: Cleopatra was Greek — Macedonian by blood. History is rarely simple.
questions.wrongReaction: Even historians fall here. Cleopatra was of Greek descent.
questions.trapText: Even historians fall here. Cleopatra was of Greek descent.

questions.text: I am built to last forever, I point to the sky, I guard a king's sleep. What am I?
questions.correctReaction: The pyramid. Built for eternity. You may pass.
questions.wrongReaction: The stones mock your ignorance. A pyramid guards the king's sleep.
questions.trapText: The stones mock your ignorance. A pyramid guards the king's sleep.

chambers.name: Chamber of the Ankh
chambers.subtitle: God of Life
chambers.introText:
The Ankh is the key to eternal life.
Osiris has granted you audience.
Prove your knowledge of the divine — or join the departed.
chambers.completeText: Osiris smiles upon you. The Ankh turns. You may pass.

questions.text: Who was the Egyptian god of the sun?
questions.correctReaction: Ra. The great light. Life itself.
questions.wrongReaction: You do not know the sun god? Osiris weeps for you.
questions.trapText: You do not know the sun god? Osiris weeps for you.

questions.text: Isis was the goddess of magic and motherhood.
questions.correctReaction: Isis. Protector, healer, mother. You know her well.
questions.wrongReaction: Isis herself would correct you. She is goddess of magic and motherhood.
questions.trapText: Isis herself would correct you. She is goddess of magic and motherhood.

questions.text: Which god had the head of a jackal?
questions.correctReaction: Anubis. The guardian of the dead. He watches this very corridor.
questions.wrongReaction: A jackal guards the dead. That is Anubis. Remember this.
questions.trapText: A jackal guards the dead. That is Anubis. Remember this.

questions.text: Set was considered a god of chaos and storms.
questions.correctReaction: Set. God of chaos, desert, and storms. Correct.
questions.wrongReaction: Set's chaos reaches even here. He was indeed god of storms.
questions.trapText: Set's chaos reaches even here. He was indeed god of storms.

questions.text: I have the head of a bird and carry a feather that weighs a soul. Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: Thoth. Ibis-headed scribe of the gods. The feather of truth is his to carry.
questions.wrongReaction: Thoth — ibis-headed god who records the weighing of souls.
questions.trapText: Thoth — ibis-headed god who records the weighing of souls.

chambers.name: Chamber of the Scarab
chambers.subtitle: God of Creation
chambers.introText:
The scarab rolls the sun across the sky.
Khepri, the creator, tests your knowledge of stone and form.
Only the architect of wisdom may proceed.
chambers.completeText: Khepri rises. The scarab grants passage. Well built, explorer.

questions.text: What is the name of the largest pyramid in Egypt?
questions.correctReaction: The Great Pyramid of Giza. The mightiest of them all.
questions.wrongReaction: The Great Pyramid of Giza towers above all others. Learn your monuments.
questions.trapText: The Great Pyramid of Giza towers above all others. Learn your monuments.

questions.text: The Sphinx at Giza has the body of a lion.
questions.correctReaction: Lion's body, human head. Power and wisdom combined. Correct.
questions.wrongReaction: The Sphinx has a lion's body. Look more carefully next time.
questions.trapText: The Sphinx has a lion's body. Look more carefully next time.

questions.text: What material were most Egyptian obelisks made from?
questions.correctReaction: Granite. Hard as eternity. Correct.
questions.wrongReaction: Granite — the hardest stone, carved by patient hands over decades.
questions.trapText: Granite — the hardest stone, carved by patient hands over decades.

questions.text: The Great Pyramid was originally covered in white casing stones.
questions.correctReaction: White Tura limestone. It shone like a star in the desert sun.
questions.wrongReaction: White casing stones once made it gleam. Time stripped them away.
questions.trapText: White casing stones once made it gleam. Time stripped them away.

questions.text: I was built to align with the stars. I hold a king's eternal rest. Remove my capstone and I am incomplete. What am I?
questions.correctReaction: A pyramid. Aligned to Orion's Belt. The king rests within. Correct.
questions.wrongReaction: A pyramid — aligned to stars, built for eternity.
questions.trapText: A pyramid — aligned to stars, built for eternity.

chambers.name: Chamber of Bastet
chambers.subtitle: Goddess of Cats
chambers.introText:
The cat goddess purrs in the shadows.
Bastet watches with eyes that miss nothing.
Honour her with wisdom — or feel her claws.
chambers.completeText: Bastet blinks slowly. A sign of approval. You may proceed.

questions.text: What did ancient Egyptians use to preserve dead bodies?
questions.correctReaction: Natron. A sacred salt that preserved the body for eternity.
questions.wrongReaction: Natron — the salt of the gods — dried and preserved the dead.
questions.trapText: Natron — the salt of the gods — dried and preserved the dead.

questions.text: Cats were considered sacred animals in ancient Egypt.
questions.correctReaction: Sacred indeed. Bastet herself walks as a cat. You know this.
questions.wrongReaction: Bastet herself is a cat. Cats were deeply sacred here.
questions.trapText: Bastet herself is a cat. Cats were deeply sacred here.

questions.text: What crop was most important to ancient Egyptian farmers?
questions.correctReaction: Wheat. The gift of the Nile flood. Life itself in grain form.
questions.wrongReaction: Wheat fed the empire. The Nile made it possible.
questions.trapText: Wheat fed the empire. The Nile made it possible.

questions.text: Ancient Egyptians invented the 365-day calendar.
questions.correctReaction: 365 days. Based on the Nile flood and star cycles. Brilliant minds.
questions.wrongReaction: The 365-day calendar began in Egypt. Time itself was their invention.
questions.trapText: The 365-day calendar began in Egypt. Time itself was their invention.

questions.text: I flow once a year and bring life from death. Without me, Egypt is desert. What am I?
questions.correctReaction: The annual Nile flood. Black silt, fertile land, life renewed. Correct.
questions.wrongReaction: The Nile flood — called Akhet — brought rich silt and life each year.
questions.trapText: The Nile flood — called Akhet — brought rich silt and life each year.

chambers.name: Chamber of the Pharaoh
chambers.subtitle: God of Kings
chambers.introText:
The Pharaoh's chamber radiates power.
Horus — the falcon king — demands you know the rulers of Egypt.
Speak their deeds or remain here forever.
chambers.completeText: Horus spreads his wings. You have honoured the pharaohs. Go forth.

questions.text: Which pharaoh is famous for his intact tomb discovered in 1922?
questions.correctReaction: Tutankhamun. The boy king. His gold still blinds archaeologists.
questions.wrongReaction: Tutankhamun — discovered by Howard Carter in 1922 — still intact.
questions.trapText: Tutankhamun — discovered by Howard Carter in 1922 — still intact.

questions.text: Ramesses II ruled Egypt for over 60 years.
questions.correctReaction: 66 years. The Great One outlasted every enemy and every doubt.
questions.wrongReaction: Ramesses II ruled 66 years — the longest reign of any great pharaoh.
questions.trapText: Ramesses II ruled 66 years — the longest reign of any great pharaoh.

questions.text: Which pharaoh introduced monotheism — worship of only one god?
questions.correctReaction: Akhenaten. He rejected the gods and worshipped only Aten. History did not forgive him.
questions.wrongReaction: Akhenaten declared Aten the only god — a revolution erased after his death.
questions.trapText: Akhenaten declared Aten the only god — a revolution erased after his death.

questions.text: Cleopatra was the last active pharaoh of ancient Egypt.
questions.correctReaction: The last. After her, Egypt fell to Rome. An era ended.
questions.wrongReaction: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom.
questions.trapText: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom.

questions.text: I built Abu Simbel. I fought at Kadesh and called it a victory. My face appears on monuments across Egypt. Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: Ramesses the Great. Builder, warrior, self-mythologiser. You know your kings.
questions.wrongReaction: Ramesses II — Abu Simbel, Kadesh, 60 years of monuments bearing his face.
questions.trapText: Ramesses II — Abu Simbel, Kadesh, 60 years of monuments bearing his face.

chambers.name: Chamber of the Pyramid
chambers.subtitle: God of Wisdom
chambers.introText:
You approach the Chamber of Secrets.
Thoth, scribe of the gods, has written the questions in starlight.
Only true wisdom unlocks what lies ahead.
chambers.completeText: Thoth records your name in the Book of Wisdom. You have passed.

questions.text: What is the Book of the Dead?
options: A collection of magic spells for the afterlife
questions.correctAnswer: A collection of magic spells for the afterlife
questions.correctReaction: Spells for safe passage to the afterlife. Thoth wrote every word.
questions.wrongReaction: The Book of the Dead guided souls through the underworld. Magic for the dead.
questions.trapText: The Book of the Dead guided souls through the underworld. Magic for the dead.

questions.text: The Rosetta Stone helped scholars decode hieroglyphics.
questions.correctReaction: The Rosetta Stone — three scripts, one message, the key to everything.
questions.wrongReaction: Discovered in 1799, the Rosetta Stone unlocked hieroglyphics for the modern world.
questions.trapText: Discovered in 1799, the Rosetta Stone unlocked hieroglyphics for the modern world.

questions.text: What is the name of the Egyptian afterlife realm?
questions.correctReaction: Aaru — the Field of Reeds. An eternal paradise for the righteous.
questions.wrongReaction: Aaru — the Field of Reeds — was the Egyptian paradise after death.
questions.trapText: Aaru — the Field of Reeds — was the Egyptian paradise after death.

questions.text: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only ancient Wonder of the World still standing.
questions.correctReaction: The only one. 4,500 years and still standing. Nothing touches it.
questions.wrongReaction: All other ancient Wonders are gone. Only the Great Pyramid endures.
questions.trapText: All other ancient Wonders are gone. Only the Great Pyramid endures.

questions.text: I am weighed against a feather. If I am heavy with sin I am devoured. If I am light I enter paradise. What am I?
questions.correctReaction: The heart. Weighed by Anubis against the feather of Ma'at. Truth is lighter than sin.
questions.wrongReaction: The heart was weighed against Ma'at's feather. Too heavy meant Ammit consumed it.
questions.trapText: The heart was weighed against Ma'at's feather. Too heavy meant Ammit consumed it.

chambers.name: Chamber of Anubis
chambers.subtitle: God of Death
chambers.introText:
You have made it this far, little explorer.
I am Anubis. I weigh the hearts of the dead.
Answer correctly... and you may leave with yours.
chambers.completeText: Anubis nods. Your heart is lighter than the feather. You may pass into the light.

questions.text: What were the four canopic jars used for?
questions.correctReaction: Four jars. Four organs. Each protected by a son of Horus. You know the ritual.
questions.wrongReaction: Canopic jars held the liver, lungs, stomach, and intestines of the mummified dead.
questions.trapText: Canopic jars held the liver, lungs, stomach, and intestines of the mummified dead.

questions.text: The process of mummification took approximately 70 days.
questions.correctReaction: 70 days. A sacred number. The time of Sirius's absence from the sky.
questions.wrongReaction: 70 days — matching the time the star Sirius disappeared from the Egyptian sky.
questions.trapText: 70 days — matching the time the star Sirius disappeared from the Egyptian sky.

questions.text: What was the name of the monster that devoured unworthy hearts in the Hall of Ma'at?
questions.correctReaction: Ammit. Part lion, part hippopotamus, part crocodile. She waits even now.
questions.wrongReaction: Ammit — the Devourer — consumed hearts too heavy with sin. She is watching.
questions.trapText: Ammit — the Devourer — consumed hearts too heavy with sin. She is watching.

questions.text: Ancient Egyptians believed the soul had five separate components.
questions.correctReaction: Five parts. Ka, Ba, Ib, Ren, and Sheut. You understand the soul.
questions.wrongReaction: Five components — Ka (life force), Ba (personality), Ib (heart), Ren (name), Sheut (shadow).
questions.trapText: Five components — Ka (life force), Ba (personality), Ib (heart), Ren (name), Sheut (shadow).

questions.text: I guard the gate between life and death. I weigh your deeds against truth itself. I am neither cruel nor kind — only just. Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: Anubis. Me. You stand before me now. You have answered well. You may pass.
questions.wrongReaction: Anubis guards the dead. He is neither merciful nor cruel — only honest.
questions.trapText: Anubis guards the dead. He is neither merciful nor cruel — only honest.
